There’s a new ponzi-style game launching on Solana in the middle of the bear market 🎮

I looked into how it works. Here’s the @cashcitydotfun  mechanics breakdown:

• You are a CEO managing up to 15 trading Firms

Your goal:
- hire Interns
- boost them with Advisors and Consumables
- compete against other players for a share of the daily $CASH token emissions

• Core Game Loop

1. Buy a Firm: Costs 1 SOL, gives you 8 desk slots, 1 Advisor slot, and 1 free Intern Pack

2. Open Packs: Use $CASH to buy Intern, Advisor, and Consumable packs (each has different rarity and impact)

3. Seat & Assign: Place Interns at desks to generate base Efficiency. Assign Advisor for a global multiplier. Apply Consumables to Interns for 24h boosts

4. Earn $CASH: Every 15-minute epoch, the network distributes $CASH based on your share of the total network Efficiency

5. Claim & Upgrade: Claim your $CASH, tokens can be used to buy more packs, upgrade Firms, or acquire better Advisors to grow your share

• Economy

– $CASH has no fixed supply cap
– New tokens are minted only when players claim emissions
– All in-game spending (packs, upgrades, etc.) is permanently burned

The team states there are no team, VC, or investor allocations.

Of course there is a referral system in place. If you choose to participate and need a code, mine is 'LZBD-H9JE'

Only risk what you can afford to lose, play responsibly and have fun.

If you're wondering why $JUP has only gone down over the past two years…

Just 8 days ago someone dumped 2.5M $JUP again

I can’t say for sure whether it was the team or Mercurial stakers

I also don't want to judge

These unlocks were known, and realistically 99% of us might have done the same in their position

But there were plenty of wallets like this — and they were blatantly dumping on the market

They didn’t even bother using Meteora to exit DCA. They clearly didn’t care about eating $5K in slippage

Even though this is a strong argument for Option 2, I still voted for Option 1 — mainly because I don’t think Option 2 gives us guaranteed upside

I also expect crypto to go lower from here

If BTC drops to $60K, no one is rushing in to buy our bags. At that point, it won’t matter whether $JUP is at $0.14 or $0.07

And in the end it might punishes users for mistakes the team made (tokenomics)

I still believe there is a big future for $JUP but probably not today or tomorrow

Visibility has consequences.

Every public transaction is data > Data becomes patterns > Patterns become targets.

📊Solana Data Insights – Over 81% of Whale Voting Weight Backs Zero Emissions in Jupiter DAO Vote

✍️ @ario_57_

Key takeaways from this report:

•⁠ ⁠More than 24.5K wallets have voted in the ongoing Jupiter DAO proposal deciding between the 700M $JUP Jupuary airdrop or a Zero Net-New Emissions model.

•⁠ ⁠By raw vote count, 13K+ wallets support proceeding with Jupuary, signaling strong retail participation.

•⁠ ⁠However, when measured by voting weight, Zero Net-New Emissions controls 73.9%+ of total stake, giving it a decisive lead.

•⁠ ⁠Wallets staking over 1M $JUP allocated 81.7% of their voting weight toward halting emissions and introducing treasury-funded buybacks.

•⁠ ⁠Smaller wallets (under 1K $JUP voting weight) are disproportionately backing the airdrop continuation.

•⁠ ⁠The proposal would pause new emissions, accelerate remaining vesting, and offset supply through buybacks, aiming to neutralize net token inflation.

•⁠ ⁠Approximately 182M of 350M Mercurial-related $JUP tokens (52%) have already vested, with 168M still unlocking.

•⁠ ⁠The top 10 voting wallets control 22.5%+ of total voting weight, highlighting concentration within token-weighted governance.

•⁠ ⁠One wallet holding 27.7M staked $JUP represents the single largest voter, intensifying debate around governance fairness vs. economic exposure alignment.

Subscribe for the full breakdown 👇
https://t.co/kCu64uo5JL
